name: Los Callejones de Las Majadas Loop
name_original: Las Majadas — Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca — circular de gravel
description: Explore the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca on this moderate 18.9-mile gravel cycling route, featuring unique rock formations at Las
region_intro: Touring cycling around Las Majadas offers routes through the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, characterized by its karstic rock formations and dense pine forests. The region features a landscape shaped by ravines and gorges, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation changes are common, with routes traversing both valleys and higher ground. This area presents a natural environment with minimal car traffic, enhancing the cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Las Majadas The most popular touring cycling route is Uña y Las Majadas — circular desde Villalba de la Sierra, a 38.5 miles (61.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Las Majadas — Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca — circular de gravel, a moderate 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. This route offers a gravel cycling experience through the natural park. Local touring cyclists also love the Beamud from Uña — circular by the Embalse de la Toba, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through the area around the Embalse de la Toba, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Las Majadas is defined by karstic rock formations, extensive pine forests, and deep ravines. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Las Majadas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 6 reviews. 135 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Majadas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Las Majadas?
A: There are nearly 20 touring cycling routes around Las Majadas, offering a variety of experiences through the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. These routes are rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Las Majadas?
A: The terrain around Las Majadas is characterized by dramatic karstic rock formations, extensive pine forests, and deep ravines. You'll find a mix of varied surfaces, including some unpaved segments, with routes traversing both valleys and higher ground, ensuring elevation changes are common.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different fitness levels?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes in Las Majadas cater to various fitness levels. While there are 7 moderate routes, the majority (11) are classified as difficult, often featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances. For a moderate gravel experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095090297/las-majadas-parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-de-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Majadas — Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca — circular de gravel&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the unique rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Callejones of Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;, visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. Panoramic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking vistas of the Júcar River canyon and the surrounding natural park.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Las Majadas is a great starting point for longer rides. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363989897/nacimiento-del-rio-cuervo-circular-desde-villalba-de-la-sierra-por-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Cuervo — circular desde Villalba de la Sierra por Uña&lt;/a&gt; extend over 100 kilometers, offering extensive exploration of the Serranía de Cuenca. The region is also part of developing long-distance routes like the 'Castellano-Manchega Cycle Tourist Route'.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Las Majadas unique?
A: Las Majadas offers a unique cycling experience due to its dramatic geological formations, such as the karstic rock landscapes of Los Callejones, and dense pine forests. The low population density and minimal car traffic allow for a peaceful immersion in nature, with opportunities to spot diverse wildlife like deer and wild boars.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Las Majadas?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364001313/una-y-las-majadas-circular-desde-villalba-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña y Las Majadas — circular desde Villalba de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084230269/beamud-from-una-circular-by-the-embalse-de-la-toba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beamud from Uña — circular by the Embalse de la Toba&lt;/a&gt;, which leads around the Embalse de la Toba.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Las Majadas?
A: The mountain climate supports dense forests, and spring brings vibrant wildflowers, hawthorns, and wild roses, making it a beautiful time to visit. Aromatic plants like lavender and thyme also fill the air. While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features like waterfalls or lakes?
A: Yes, the region features seasonal waterfalls such as Cascada Dehesa de los Olmos and Cascada del Fraile. Additionally, routes often pass by the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the Embalse de la Toba, offering picturesque views and refreshing stops.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Las Majadas?
A: The routes in Las Majadas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the immersive experience in nature, and the quiet roads with minimal car traffic, making it a peaceful destination for touring.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the dramatic gorges of the region?
A: Absolutely. The landscape is shaped by barrancos (ravines) and gorges. While cycling, you can experience the dramatic scenery around viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning views into the Júcar River canyon.
tips: Los Callejones de Las Majadas is a landscape of curious rock formations that have been formed by erosion. It is a very simple route of about 4 km that can be easily done in 1.5 hours. The trail is very well marked. Admission is free.
The Tío Cogote Viewpoint is one of the most spectacular viewpoints that can be found in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Las Majadas — Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca — circular de gravel route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It's suitable for touring cyclists with good fitness. While it features mostly paved sections, some unpaved segments can be challenging and require appropriate bike handling skills for gravel surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel route?
faqs.answer: The route is designated as a gravel trail, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter mostly paved sections, but also unpaved segments that traverse dirt tracks. The broader Serranía de Cuenca region is known for its limestone rock formations, ravines, and river gorges, so expect varied and scenic ground.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is set within the stunning Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca, renowned for its impressive landscapes. A significant highlight is Los Callejones de Las Majadas, where millennia of erosion have sculpted limestone rocks into unique shapes, forming natural passages and arches. You can also enjoy panoramic views, with nearby viewpoints like Tío Cogote Viewpoint offering vistas of the Júcar River canyon.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca region generally offers a pleasant cycling experience. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters may bring colder weather, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this gravel route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Las Majadas is a small village within the natural park. You can typically find parking in or near the village center, which serves as a common starting point for routes in the area. The natural park generally has low population density and minimal car traffic.

faqs.question: Is the Las Majadas — Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca — circular de gravel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Please ensure your dog is comfortable with the 30.5 km distance and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling on established routes within the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Ruta de Las Fuentecillas y Los Callejones, camino de la Fuente de la Zarza, Sendero de Los Callejones de las Majadas, and camino de Las Coronillas.

faqs.question: Is this gravel route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, 30.5 km distance, and some challenging unpaved segments, this specific route might be too demanding for younger children or families new to gravel cycling. Families with older, experienced teenage cyclists who have good fitness might find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What makes this route a good choice for gravel cycling?
faqs.answer: This route is an excellent choice for gravel cycling due to its location within the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca, which is recognized as a 'cycling paradise.' It offers a mix of paved and well-maintained dirt tracks, stunning natural scenery, and the unique geological formations of Los Callejones de Las Majadas, all with minimal car traffic.

faqs.question: What is the elevation gain on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features an elevation gain of approximately 362 meters (1,188 feet) and a similar descent. This moderate elevation profile contributes to the 'moderate' difficulty rating and offers some rewarding climbs and descents.
